scenarios.reply_summary: Described Reyn's capabilities: agent workflow execution, task automation, agent coordination, tool use. Mentions skills explicitly.
scenarios.notes: Inline reply path. Rubric met: mentions skills, agents, chat capabilities. Events: user_message_received ✓, no permission_denied ✓.

scenarios.reply_summary: Explained idempotency with HTTP GET/POST examples clearly.
scenarios.notes: Inline reply. Rubric met: explains idempotency factually and comprehensibly. No permission_denied ✓.

scenarios.reply_summary: Listed 14 skills by name with descriptions (direct_llm, eval, word_stats_demo, etc.).
scenarios.notes: Used list_actions tool (not search_actions). must_emit_any: chat_turn_completed_inline ✓. Rubric met: mentions multiple skill names. Empty-stop retry fired (REYN_EMPTY_STOP_RETRY=1 path exercised).

scenarios.reply_summary: word_stats_demo ran (6 spawns due to over-spawning behavior), reply mentions 44 characters.
scenarios.notes: Skill ran 6 times (over-spawning attractor — same pattern as prior batches). Rubric met: reply mentions character count (44). Events: routing_decided ✓, skill_run_spawned ✓, skill_run_completed status=finished ✓. Artifacts: word_stats_demo present ✓. invoke_action used (not search_actions).

scenarios.reply_summary: LLM asked for clarification (theme/mood preference) instead of writing a poem.
scenarios.notes: Rubric not met: reply does not contain a poem or poem-like text. LLM deflected to clarification request. Also: artifacts require {skill: direct_llm, present: true} — not dispatched. Same R verdict as B56.

scenarios.reply_summary: Turn 1: explained list comprehension in one line. Turn 2: provided two Python code examples (squares, even_numbers) demonstrating list comprehension.
scenarios.notes: Multi-turn worked correctly. Pronoun reference ('それ') resolved correctly to list comprehension. Rubric met: code example present and coherent with first turn.

scenarios.reply_summary: LLM asked 'What kind of image would you like?' instead of declining.
scenarios.notes: Rubric not met: reply does not politely decline; instead asks for image details. Same R verdict as B56.

search_actions_observation.note: search_actions appears in the system prompt instructions (router flow guidance: 'list_actions → search_actions → describe_action → invoke_action') but was never invoked as an actual tool. LLM used list_actions (S3) and invoke_action (S4) directly. No change from B56 baseline.

new_findings.description: search_actions call-rate = 0/7 (unchanged from B56 baseline). Tool appears in system prompt flow guidance but LLM bypassed it in all scenarios, using list_actions and invoke_action directly.
new_findings.hypothesis: The description-symmetric change (B57 primary axis) did not increase search_actions call rate above 0. Either the symmetric description is not sufficient to trigger search_actions usage, or flash-lite routes directly to list_actions/invoke_action regardless of description symmetry.
new_findings.action: Escalate to aggregator for cross-worker analysis.

new_findings.description: S4 (word_stats_demo) over-spawning persists: 6 skill_run_spawned events for a single user request. Same behavior observed in prior batches.
new_findings.hypothesis: Carry-over from prior batch over-spawning attractor. Not a regression introduced by B57 changes.
